title: carya ovata var. mexicana
intro: Carya ovata var. mexicana, also known as Mexican hickory, is a variety of the Shagbark Hickory tree (Carya ovata) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering benefits like relief from inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. The Mexican hickory can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
